p-conversations.title: Conversations Archive
p-conversations.desc: An ongoing archive of conversations with a generation of designers and artists working at the edges of the fashion system. Conversations Archive gathers the interview practice at the core of C19's editorial work: long-form conversations with emerging designers and artists, conducted, edited and laid out by Cyana-Djoher Hadjali. The series treats the interview as primary material, a way of documenting design gestures and positions before institutions do. For Newcoin Research Center, the archive extended internationally with interviews of Tomikono Wigg, Louisa Gagliardi, Vitalii Akiniov and J.Kim; covering editorial coordination, layouts and talent submission across fashion, art and Web3.

p-middaia.title: Middaia: Wholesale Development
p-middaia.desc: Building the wholesale architecture of an independent label – from linesheet to retailer negotiation. C19 leads wholesale development for Middaia, the label founded by Jade Nguyen Van: market positioning, seasonal wholesale strategy, buyer outreach and retailer negotiation, including development toward the Asian market. The scope extends to press and event support – trunk show press materials and outreach – treating wholesale not as pure distribution but as the narrative through which a young brand becomes legible to buyers. The objective: a distribution network that matches the label's pace, rather than forcing the label to match the market's.

p-enami.title: ENAMI: Brand Management & Wholesale
p-enami.desc: Full-scope brand development: identity, structure and wholesale for an emerging house. For ENAMI, C19 operates as an embedded brand management partner – shaping identity and positioning, structuring product architecture, and building the wholesale strategy that carries the brand to market. The work spans strategic definition and daily operations: brand development, market positioning and buyer-facing materials, with wholesale treated as the proof of concept of the brand's identity.

p-form.title: FORM: Fashion Archive Development
p-form.desc: Leading the development of a fashion archive platform – sourcing, structuring and narrating designer histories. As Archive Lead at FORM, Cyana-Djoher Hadjali is also the host of Form Academy, a video series on fashion designers drawn from her PhD research corpus – combining biographical analysis with close readings of collections and runway shows.

p-acne.title: VIP & Celebrity Coordination
p-acne.desc: Multi-year engagement coordinating VIP and celebrity guests for the Acne Studios show at Paris Fashion Week (September 2023, February 2024, March 2026): transfers, hair and make-up scheduling, fittings, welcome packs, guest hospitality and show-day arrivals. Preceded by eighteen months within the Events & Projects department (2022–2023): event production from sourcing to breakdown, budget tracking, call sheets and retro-planning, SS23 show assistance and front-of-house operations, including coordinating the press trip to Kero AB in Pajala and Stockholm for the launch of the Acne Studios Kero Boots.

p-messika.title: Press & Guest Relations
p-messika.desc: Embedded within the press and PR team for the Messika show during Paris Fashion Week: guest list management, invitation logistics and calligraphy coordination, seating plan support, photographer accreditations and press check-ins, and live liaison with PRs and their media on show day.

p-puig-creo.title: Creative Hub (CREO): Copywriting & Content Curation
p-puig-creo.desc: Editorial copywriting and web content curation for the Puig Creative Hub, in French and English: fragrance and beauty storytelling, city and cultural guides, trend-focused content, and coordination of content and visual materials across the group's cultural programming.

p-carhartt.title: Copywriting
p-carhartt.desc: Two collaborations with Carhartt WIP: creative writing for WIP.08, and a 2026 French product copywriting mission covering the full seasonal range – technical garment vocabulary translated into precise, brand-true French.

p-cartier.title: Cover Shoot Production
p-cartier.desc: Production and project management for the Harper's Bazaar Turkey × Cartier cover shoot in Paris – coordinating talent, locations, logistics and on-set operations for an international editorial team.

p-ashaalia.title: Creative Direction, Strategy & Wholesale
p-ashaalia.desc: Ongoing creative direction and strategic consulting for Ashaalia: brand audit and positioning, product copy and copy guidelines, e-commerce structure, and wholesale outreach – building the operational and narrative infrastructure of the brand.

p-palladium.title: SS22 'Ticket to Earth'
p-palladium.desc: Creative writing and voice-over for Palladium's SS22 campaign 'Ticket to Earth', marking the brand's 75th anniversary.

p-newcoin.title: Editor, Newcoin Research Center
p-newcoin.desc: Cultural trend analysis and editorial strategy at the intersection of fashion, aesthetics and Web3 – interviews with international designers and artists, editorial layout and narrative direction for the Newcoin Research Center.

p-lemindu.title: 'Demons': Silicone Masks
p-lemindu.desc: Creation of silicone masks for the music video 'Demons' by Angèle ft. Damso, in collaboration with Charlie Le Mindu.

p-premiercri.title: Brand Development, Creative Strategy, Production
p-premiercri.desc: Website development, social media strategy, art direction and copywriting – plus the organisation of launch events, screenings and concert evenings.

p-unhome.title: UNHOME, Paris Edition
p-unhome.desc: Research and writing for a nomadic design retail concept, alongside PR invitations and guest coordination. From manifesto to event, design for the real home.

services.name: Creative Strategy Consulting
services.desc: Positioning, narrative architecture, and cultural and art direction for fashion brands and cultural institutions. Drawing on more than five years of experience across the fashion industry, C19 combines fashion theory, market intelligence and trend analysis to translate ideas into clear, actionable strategies that creative teams can execute.

services.name: Art Direction Consulting
services.desc: Visual identity, image direction and iconographic research for campaigns, editorials, publications and brand worlds. Each visual system is developed through archival research, reference building and a deep understanding of fashion history, visual culture and contemporary image-making.

services.name: Marketing, Storytelling & Wholesale
services.desc: Wholesale strategy and business development for independent fashion brands, spanning market positioning, pricing architecture, brand books, line sheets, buyer outreach, and agency and showroom strategy. C19 helps translate a brand's identity into a coherent commercial narrative that resonates with buyers, retailers and industry partners.

services.name: Brand Management
services.desc: Full-scope brand development for emerging houses: pitch decks, identity, product architecture, copy guidelines, operational structure. Building the infrastructure a brand needs before it scales.

services.name: Production & Celebrity Coordination
services.desc: Show and event production, press and guest relations, VIP and celebrity coordination – fittings, transfers, welcome packs, seating, show-day arrivals. Calm under Fashion Week pressure.

services.name: Editorial Curation & Copywriting
services.desc: Editorial strategy, research and content development for brands, publications and cultural institutions. From interviews, long-form writing and copywriting to research dossiers, presentations and editorial narratives, C19 develops content that is rigorous, engaging and tailored to its audience.

writing.title: Nietzsche's Shadow in Ecological Thought
writing.summary: Genealogy of morality and the construction of a future ethics in post-apocalyptic science-fiction worlds. This essay traces Nietzsche's shadow across ecological thought – reading post-apocalyptic science fiction as a laboratory where the genealogy of morality is re-run under conditions of collapse, and where a future ethics is constructed from the ruins of the present one.

writing.title: 032c: Sara Choi
writing.summary: A conversation with Sara Choi for 032c. Written for 032c, this piece profiles Sara Choi – her practice, references and position within the current design landscape.

writing.title: Grills: Smiling at the System
writing.venue: Cara Magazine, Mouth Issue, n°03 (year to be confirmed)
writing.summary: Grills, une dent contre le système – another politics of the smile. Published in Cara Magazine's Mouth Issue, this bilingual essay dives into the history of dental adornment – from Mayan gemstone inlays to Eddie Plein's custom grillz – reading grills through Barthes' fashion system as ornaments that politicise the mouth: part jewellery, part armour, a smile turned against the codes of dominant beauty.

writing.title: FERAL: Selected Writings
writing.summary: Founder & Editor-in-Chief. A monthly digital publication on speculative fashion ecologies, aesthetics and cultural narratives, born from the platform LazyF4shiongurl (2023). Issue 01 'A Fetish for Hybrids', Issue 02 'Enclothed Intimacy & Design Positivity'.

writing.title: Material Memories and Internal Ecologies: Reconnecting Fashion to Cultural Continuity Through Boro in High Fashion
writing.venue: 28th IFFTI Annual Conference, RMIT Vietnam, Ho Chi Minh City
writing.summary: Conference paper reading boro as material memory and a form of cultural continuity within high fashion.

writing.title: Transparent Garments and Image-Clothing: On the Digitalization of Fashion and the Loss of Enclothed Knowledge
writing.venue: 16th International Conference on the Image, Université Paris 1 Panthéon-Sorbonne. Selected as Emerging Scholar
writing.summary: Conference paper on the digitalization of fashion, transparent garments and the loss of enclothed knowledge.

writing.title: The Revival of the Japanese Technique of Boro in High Fashion: Mending, Materiality, and the Ecology of Repair
writing.venue: CIMAD, International Conference on Art and Design Practices, Saint-Étienne & Lyon (forthcoming in proceedings)
writing.summary: Conference paper on boro, mending, materiality and the ecology of repair in high fashion.

writing.title: Rebuilding Fashion through Subversion: Punk, Grunge, and Politics of Deconstruction in Luxury Ready-to-Wear
writing.venue: Nouvelles Gestualités Dissidentes symposium, ENSAD Limoges & Université Paris 1
writing.summary: Symposium paper on punk, grunge and the politics of deconstruction in luxury ready-to-wear.

writing.title: Gestures and Temporalities of Fashion
writing.venue: Study day, Institut Français de la Mode, co-organised with Jeanne-Léopoldine Claustre and Estelle Dupuis
writing.summary: A co-organised study day bringing together designers, archivists, and researchers to examine how the fashion gesture is repeated, transmitted, and altered over time. Chaired a practitioner discussion with Tim Wirth and Igor Dieryck (Dior Homme).

writing.title: On the Sustainability of the Fashion Design Gesture
writing.venue: Doctoral research, Université Paris 1 Panthéon-Sorbonne / Università di Bologna, under Barbara Formis and Stefano Marino, IFM Doctoral Research Programme
writing.summary: Ongoing doctoral research investigating ecological aesthetics, anti-fashion, material cultures and the structure of the fashion system in the Anthropocene.

site.heroHeadline: C19 Consulting is a Paris-based creative consultancy providing end-to-end support for fashion, luxury and cultural brands. Founded by PhD candidate Cyana-Djoher Anaïs Hadjali, the studio combines strategic thinking, creative direction and research-led methodologies to build brands with long-term cultural relevance.
site.heroAuthor: C19 Consulting - 360° Creative Strategy Marketing & Wholesale,  Archive & Editorial Research · Paris & Tokyo
site.aboutIntro: A consulting practice between strategy and scholarship. Creative direction, wholesale, editorial, archive  and production – Paris/Tokyo
site.aboutPara1:
C19 Consulting is a Paris-based creative consultancy providing end-to-end support for fashion, luxury and cultural brands. Founded by PhD candidate in Philosophy & Fashion Theory Cyana-Djoher Anaïs Hadjali (Université Paris 1 Panthéon-Sorbonne / Università di Bologna), the studio combines strategic thinking, creative direction and research-led methodologies to build brands with long-term cultural relevance.

The consultancy works across creative and marketing strategy, brand management, wholesale development, production, editorial research, archive development, partnerships and bespoke consulting.
site.aboutPara2: Grounded in research and an extensive knowledge of fashion history, design and visual culture, every project is conceived as a coherent ecosystem where strategy, creativity and execution reinforce one another.
